zh.Roslin, Midlothian, Scotland: 蘇格蘭米德洛迪安洛斯林
zh.lifeSummary: 當William Sinclair於大約1045年出生在法國諾門迪沙吉利o時，他的父親Waldron de Sinclair28歲，母親Helena Of Normandie19歲。他於1060年之前與Dorothy Agnes Dunbar在蘇格蘭結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。他去世於大約1098年在英國蘇格蘭享年55歲，埋葬在蘇格蘭米德洛迪安洛斯林。

zh-hans.Roslin, Midlothian, Scotland: 苏格兰米德洛迪安洛斯林
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William Sinclair于大约1045年出生在法国諾門迪沙吉利o时，他的父亲Waldron de Sinclair28岁，母亲Helena Of Normandie19岁。他于1060年之前与Dorothy Agnes Dunbar在苏格兰结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。他去世于大约1098年在英国苏格兰享年55岁，埋葬在苏格兰米德洛迪安洛斯林。

nameMeanings.name: Sinclair
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish and English: of Norman origin, a habitational name from Saint-Clair-sur-Elle (Manche), so called from the dedication of its church to Saint Clarus (see   Clare   3).
Americanized form of some similar (like-sounding) Jewish surname.

History: The Scottish family Sinclair (see 1 above) came to southern Scotland in the twelfth century from Huntingdonshire with the de Morvilles. Descendants of these Sinclairs became earls of Caithness in the 15th century. The frequency of the surname in Caithness and Orkney is largely due to the adoption by tenants of the name of their overlord.

variants.name: Clare
meanings: Irish (Clare, Wicklow, and Wexford): habitational name from Clare in Suffolk (an eastern county of England), the center of a major Norman baronial estate since the Norman Conquest. The English placename probably originated as a Celtic river name meaning ‘bright, gentle, or warm’. The surname is not from the Irish county name Clare, which is either from the surname or from a place called with Gaelic clár ‘plank bridge’. Another Norman surname in the southeast of Ireland was de Clere, from Clères in Seine-Maritime (France), named from the local river.

English and French: from the Middle English, Old French female personal name Cla(i)re (from Latin Clara, from clarus ‘clear, bright, famous’) which achieved some popularity, greater elsewhere in Europe than in England, through the fame of Saint Clare of Assisi (see Chiara ). The English surname seems to have been interchangeable with Clear 1.

English: occupational name from an agent derivative of Middle English cley ‘clay’. The work of medieval clayers involved plastering a framework of interwoven twigs with mud to produce wattle-and-daub work.

variants.name: St. Clair
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Sinclair  . This is also a French Canadian and American French altered form of the same surname.
French (Saint-Clair): habitational name from any of several places, e.g. in Normandy, named Saint-Clair (from the dedication of their churches to Saint Clarus). Compare Saintclair .

Haitian (also Saint-Clair): ornamental name or nickname, perhaps ironic, composed of French saint ‘saint’ or ‘holy’ and clair ‘clear’ or ‘lightness’. Alternatively, perhaps of the same French origin as 2 above. Compare Saintclair .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: When William Sinclair was born about 1045, in Saint-Lô, Normandy, France, his father, Waldron de Sinclair, was 28 and his mother, Helena Of Normandie, was 19. He married Dorothy Agnes Dunbar before 1060, in Scotland. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. He died about 1098, in Scotland, United Kingdom, at the age of 55, and was buried in Roslin, Midlothian, Scotland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William Sinclair est né environ 1045, à Saint-Lô, Normandie, France, son père, Waldron de Sinclair, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Helena Of Normandie, avait 19 ans. Il a épousé Dorothy Agnes Dunbar avant 1060, à Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Il est décédé environ 1098, à Écosse, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 55 ans, et a été enterré à Roslin, Midlothian, Écosse.

de.lifeSummary: Als William Sinclair um 1045 in Saint-Lô, Normandie, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Waldron de Sinclair 28 und seine Mutter Helena Of Normandie 19. Er hat vor 1060 in Scotland Dorothy Agnes Dunbar geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne. Er starb um 1098 im Alter von 55 Jahren in Schottland, United Kingdom und wurde in Roslin, Midlothian, Scotland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando William Sinclair è nato il circa 1045 nel Saint-Lô, Normandie, Francia, il suo padre, Waldron de Sinclair, aveva 28 anni e il suo madre, Helena Of Normandie, aveva 19 anni. Lui sposato Dorothy Agnes Dunbar prima del 1060, nel Scotland. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli. Morì circa 1098, nel Scozia, Regno Unito, all'età di 55 anni, e fu sepolto a Roslin, Midlothian, Scotland.

ja.St Lo, Normandy, France: フランスノーマンディサイント-ルo
ja.lifeSummary: William Sinclairが約1045年にフランスノーマンディサイント-ルoで生まれた時，父Waldron de Sinclairは28歳，母Helena Of Normandieは19歳であった。1060年以前にスコットランドでDorothy Agnes Dunbarと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子の親であった。彼は約1098年にイギリススコットランドで55歳で死去し，スコットランドミドロスアンロスリンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: William Sinclair(이)가 대략 1045년 프랑스 노먼디 세인트-o에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Waldron de Sinclair(은)는 28세였고, 그의 어머니 Helena Of Normandie(은)는 19세였다. 그는 1060년 이전 스컷랜드에서 Dorothy Agnes Dunbar(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 대략 1098년 영국 스코틀랜드에서 55세의 나이로 사망했으며 스컷랜드 미들로스이언 로슬린에 묻혔다.

mg.Abt 1045–Abt 1098: tany ho any amin'ny 1045–tany ho any amin'ny 1098

pt.lifeSummary: Quando William Sinclair nasceu aproximadamente 1045, em Saint-Lô, Normandie, França, seu pai, Waldron de Sinclair, tinha 28 anos e sua mãe, Helena Of Normandie, tinha 19 anos. Ele casou-se com Dorothy Agnes Dunbar antes 1060, em Scotland. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos. Ele faleceu aproximadamente 1098, em Escócia, United Kingdom, com 55 anos, e foi sepultado em Roslin, Midlothian, Scotland.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William Sinclair родился около 1045 г. в местности сеинт-лo, норманди, Франция, его отцу по имени Waldron de Sinclair было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Helena Of Normandie, 19. Он женился на женщине по имени Dorothy Agnes Dunbar перед 1060 г. в местности Шотландия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Он умер около 1098 г. в местности Шотландия, Великобритания в возрасте 55 лет и был похоронен в местности рослинь, мидлотян, Шотландия.

sm.Abt 1045–Abt 1098: e tusa o le 1045–e tusa o le 1098

es.lifeSummary: Cuando William Sinclair nació aproximadamente 1045, en Saint-Lô, Normandie, Francia, su papá, Waldron de Sinclair, tenía 28 años y su mamá, Helena Of Normandie, tenía 19 años. Se casó con Dorothy Agnes Dunbar antes de 1060, en Scotland. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos. Murió aproximadamente 1098, en Escocia, Reino Unido, a la edad de 55 años, y fue sepultado en Roslin, Midlothian, Scotland.
